## [Note]

dbbot v0.3.0 is the next official release after v0.1.0 and introduces a managed control-host upgrade path for curated release-package users.

## [Add]

- Add the root-level `dbbotctl` lifecycle CLI with `version`, `doctor`, `env setup`, `release history`, `release upgrade`, and `release rollback` subcommands.
- Add release-package upgrade and rollback documentation for online and offline control-host workflows.

## [Change]

- Include `bin/dbbotctl` and `libexec/dbbotctl/` in the curated release package so the upgrade CLI ships with official release assets.
- Keep dbbotctl runtime state under `.dbbotctl/` and exclude it from git tracking.
- Adjust the default MySQL instance directory layout so per-port data, config, and log paths are grouped under the same instance root.
- Disable jemalloc preload by default and require explicit opt-in before allowing bundled fallback behavior.

## [Fix]

- Make `setup_portable_ansible.sh` resolve its own directory so it can install `sshpass` and register aliases correctly when called outside the portable-ansible working directory.
- Run `dbbotctl env setup` from the portable-ansible home instead of relying on the caller's current directory.
- Fix latest-release tag resolution in `dbbotctl release upgrade --latest` so JSON parsing works on the control host.
- Preserve inventory, downloads, logs, and user-owned playbook config paths during package replacement while storing target-release templates for overwritten paths in the rollback snapshot.
- Verify `tar` availability on the control host before MySQL package validation during syntax-checked deployment runs.
- Refresh the bundled `exporterregistrar` binary and MySQL systemd handling for the updated allocator controls.
